So this problem where data points can stack on top of each other is called over plotting.

And it happens a lot if you have even like a moderately large data set and you try to look at a scatter plot, it is really common to have some amount of over plotting right where your points and your scatter plot are going to overlap with each other. And it gets kind of hard to know, are there like a lot of points here or is it just like a few stacked on top of each other? So how do you deal with this? There's three solutions. And.

To a period in the past.
Similar by what measure, right. Similar in terms of how the sales numbers look, similar in terms of how what the business schools are. There's a lot of different definitions of similarity.

And so a colleague of mine and I had a meeting with a data scientist from this company we were working with who was trying to solve this problem. Why? Because in a month we have to meet with a senior vice president, right? This is a fairly large company and that person is going to want to know.
